output.dependency: A shared geographic/organization identifier relationship: the identifier in one column corresponds to the named facility/organization in another (the same record uses the ID for the same hospital).
Facility location dependency: the facility name in one column determines the street address format/content in another (address belongs to that hospital).
City/state relationship: the city value in one column is the municipality within the state/province code/value in another (the postal location fields are consistent pairs).
Facility-to-city dependency: the hospital name determines the city where it is located.
State-to-zip relationship: the state value constrains the postal/ZIP code values in another column (ZIP is issued within the state shown).
ZIP-to-county/region relationship: the postal code value implies the associated county/region value (the county/region is consistent with the ZIP).

Clinical condition-to-measure code dependency: the diagnosis/condition category in one column maps to a standardized measure/denominator code (e.g., 'heart attack' aligns with specific 'ami-*' measure codes).
Code-to-definition dependency: the measure code in one column determines the specific clinical process/indicator text/description in another (the text explains what the code measures).
Condition-to-measure description dependency: the condition category in one column constrains the clinical process description in another to match that condition’s measure set.

Derived/composite measure identifier dependency: the abbreviated measure code in one column and the composite code in another use the same measure suffix (the composite appears to embed the state and measure code).
